summary: Small matching-focused PR touching fighter, game-mode, stage, interface, and library files. The bot report records +940 matched bytes, 6 new matches, and 2 unmatched-item improvements. Changes include recovered or match-forcing signatures, callsite argument reordering, HSD_ASSERT cleanup for a raw __assert, temporary locals and PAD_STACK-style codegen tactics, and header prototype updates.

key_files.role: Changed ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 from void to u8* and returned loop pointer p after widening its lifetime.
Updated ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 prototype to match the recovered u8* return type.
Adjusted gm_801674C4 locals, casts, pointer aliases, and PAD_STACK placement for a reported new match.
Changed fn_8018E618 parameter order to int, f32, int and updated multiple callsites; bot report records one new match and one improvement in this unit.
Updated fn_8018E618 prototype to the changed argument order.
Changed fn_80174920 return type to u8 with an inline caveat comment; adjusted fn_801749B8 locals and expressions for reported new matches.
Updated fn_80174920 prototype to u8 return type.
Added debug/item inline includes, introduced GET_ITEM2, replaced raw __assert with HSD_ASSERT after review prompt, renamed gobj to map_gobj, and retained PAD_STACK for fn_801FBF6C reported new match.
Changed un_802FF620 loop body to use an x8 substruct pointer temporary and removed a prior PAD_STACK/self-assignment shape; bot report records partial improvement.
Introduced a temporary pointer to lbSnap_803BACC8 for the lb_8001C4A8 argument expression.

what_changed: The bot report says matched code increased by 940 bytes, from 71.73% by +0.02%, with 6 new matched functions: fn_80174920, fn_801749B8, gm_801674C4, fn_801FBF6C, fn_8019B860, and ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420.
The bot report also lists improvements in unmatched fn_8019B458 and un_802FF620.
Function signatures and matching headers were changed for ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420, fn_8018E618, and fn_80174920.
fn_8018E618 callsites were updated to pass the f32 argument before the final int argument.
grinishie1.c replaced an explicit __assert("grinishie1.c", 0x425, "map_gobj") pattern with HSD_ASSERT(0x425, map_gobj), matching review feedback asking whether it could be an HSD_ASSERT.
Several functions used narrow codegen-shaping edits: widened local lifetimes, pointer aliases, temporary assignments, casts, PAD_STACK, and reordered declarations.

smart_moves.text: When a changed body implied a new signature, the PR updated the corresponding public header in the same patch for ftkirby.h, gm_18A5.h, and gmresult.h.
The grinishie1.c assert cleanup aligned raw assert evidence with the existing HSD_ASSERT macro form rather than preserving generated-looking assert code.
Callsite updates for fn_8018E618 were made consistently across multiple callers after changing the function's parameter order.

decomp_lessons.lesson: If matching evidence forces a signature change, update the owning header in the same PR rather than leaving a stale prototype.
A raw __assert with matching file, line, and predicate can often be expressed as HSD_ASSERT when local macro support is included.
Parameter order recovery can require updating both the callee signature and all visible callsites; in this slice fn_8018E618 changed from int,int,float to int,float,int and callsites were adjusted.

naming_conventions.convention: Use role-oriented locals when the role is visible, e.g. grinishie1.c changed a generic gobj local to map_gobj before asserting and passing it to map helpers.
This PR still contains generic match-temporary names such as new_var, new_var2, new_var3, tmp, thing, and p; treat them as local codegen artifacts rather than accepted naming style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Widen a pointer local's lifetime and return it when the function signature match requires a value, as shown by ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 changing to return p.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The slice does not include objdiff details beyond the bot report; do not generalize this as style without verifier evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use pointer aliases and temporary locals to influence register/stack shape, seen in gm_801674C4, gmresult fn_801749B8, ifcoget un_802FF620, and lbsnap lbSnap_8001DF20.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Many names are generic and appear codegen-driven; keep such tactics narrow and evidence-backed.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was retained or introduced in some matched code paths, including gm_801674C4 and fn_801FBF6C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Loaded evidence does not show adjacent regression checks, so this remains a tactic observation rather than a recommended standard.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A questionable return type can be accepted for matching in a narrow case, as gmresult.c adds the comment 'Return clearly wrong but match is match' before changing fn_80174920 from void to u8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This is explicitly suspicious in the diff comment and should not be promoted as a general style rule.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.subject: Raw __assert("grinishie1.c", 0x425, "map_gobj") in fn_801FBF6C.
review_feedback.observed_resolution: The diff shows HSD_ASSERT(0x425, map_gobj) and adds baselib/debug.h.

searchable_terms: return clearly wrong but match is match
follow_up_queries: PR2639 fn_80174920 return clearly wrong but match is match
fn_8018E618 int float int callsite parameter order gm_18A5
un_802FF620 Same issue as un_802FF570 thing x8

notes: PR body is empty, so author intent is inferred only from diff, title, and bot report.
Bot report provides strong matching evidence for named functions, but no raw build logs, objdiff screenshots, or adjacent regression checks are loaded.
The review state in text_corpus is DISMISSED; the slice still preserves the review comment and the diff shows a corresponding HSD_ASSERT change.
Several edits are visibly match-shaped and use generic temporary names; they are recorded as tactics, not accepted style.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header prototypes should track recovered bodies in matching PRs
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2639 changed recovered function signatures in C files and updated the corresponding headers for ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420, fn_8018E618, and fn_80174920.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Raw assert cleanup to HSD_ASSERT was reviewer-suggested in grinishie1
accepted_candidate_records.text: In fn_801FBF6C, review asked whether a raw __assert could be HSD_ASSERT; the diff shows HSD_ASSERT(0x425, map_gobj) with baselib/debug.h included.

source_update_candidates.title: Treat obviously match-only suspicious signatures as narrow exceptions requiring evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2639 changed fn_80174920 from void to u8 with the comment 'Return clearly wrong but match is match' and the bot report lists the function as newly matched. Curator may consider whether standards should explicitly say that suspicious recovered return types can be landed only with narrow match evidence and a cleanup trail, not as normal API truth.
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing standards cover truthful headers and evidence-led verification, but this PR exposes a specific edge case where a header-visible signature is acknowledged as likely wrong while used for a match.

source_update_candidates.title: Review raw __assert conversions to HSD_ASSERT when file, line, and predicate line up
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2639 includes reviewer feedback asking whether a raw __assert in grinishie1.c could be HSD_ASSERT, and the diff uses HSD_ASSERT(0x425, map_gobj). This supports existing assert macro guidance and may be useful as a concrete example.
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate example for the existing assert-report-macros standard; not a new rule.

rejection_notes: Do not promote generic locals such as new_var, new_var2, thing, or tmp as naming conventions; they are likely match scaffolding.
Do not infer that PAD_STACK, comma-expression address taking, or widened temporary lifetimes are broadly accepted style from this PR alone.
Do not claim full validation beyond the loaded bot report; no direct objdiff, build log, or adjacent regression evidence is present in the slice.
Do not treat the suspicious u8 return for fn_80174920 as semantic truth without additional evidence.
